old_claim.note: Electronics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: CGMs require electronic sensing, signal processing, and transmission or readout hardware to convert glucose measurements into continuous readings.

source_shape.source_locator: FDA executive summary descriptions of CGM sensors, transmitters, and mobile or receiver readout systems.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The FDA document describes CGMs as systems using sensors and electronics that transmit or present glucose readings continuously for diabetes management.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a required electronics edge because the measurement must be converted, processed, and communicated by device electronics to become continuous readings.

ontology_before: CGM electronics were modeled as a generic enabler.
ontology_after: Electronics are now modeled as required device architecture for CGM sensing and readout.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: CGM's electronics edge is required.
would_reject_if: The node were re-scoped to non-electronic chemical glucose test strips.
A source showed continuous glucose monitors can produce continuous readings without electronic sensing or readout.
The graph removed the glucose biosensor component while strengthening electronics.
short_reason: A CGM is an electronic sensor system, not only a clinical workflow.
